meta.name: LOT-R Life Orientation Test
meta.description: The LOT-R is a 10-item self-report measure (6 scored, 4 fillers) assessing dispositional optimism vs. pessimism. It is the most widely used measure of optimism as a personality trait.
meta.author: Scheier, Carver & Bridges (1994)
meta.reference: Scheier MF, Carver CS, Bridges MW. Distinguishing optimism from neuroticism (and trait anxiety, self-mastery, and self-esteem): a reevaluation of the Life Orientation Test. J Pers Soc Psychol. 1994;67(6):1063-1078.
meta.instruction: Please indicate how much you agree with each statement.

questions.text: In uncertain times, I usually expect the best
It is easy for me to relax
If something can go wrong for me, it will
I am always optimistic about my future
I enjoy my friends a lot
It is important for me to keep busy
I hardly ever expect things to go my way
I do not get upset too easily
I rarely count on good things happening to me
Overall, I expect more good things to happen to me than bad

ranges.label: Pessimistic Orientation
ranges.description: Your score suggests a predominantly pessimistic outlook. You tend to expect negative outcomes and may have difficulty seeing positive possibilities in uncertain situations. While dispositional pessimism can serve as a form of self-protection, persistent pessimism is associated with poorer health outcomes and reduced well-being.
suggestions: Consider practicing cognitive restructuring to identify and challenge habitually negative expectations
Engage in activities that build a sense of mastery and competence
Mindfulness practices can help you observe pessimistic thoughts without being controlled by them

ranges.label: Moderate Optimism
ranges.description: Your score indicates a moderate level of optimism. You have a balanced outlook, sometimes expecting positive outcomes and sometimes anticipating difficulties. This realistic perspective is common and can be adaptive.
suggestions: Continue maintaining your balanced perspective while cultivating positive expectations where possible
Practice gratitude exercises to strengthen your awareness of positive aspects in your life
Set achievable goals to build confidence and a sense of positive anticipation

ranges.label: Optimistic Orientation
ranges.description: Your score suggests a predominantly optimistic outlook. You generally expect positive outcomes and tend to see the bright side of uncertain situations. Dispositional optimism is consistently associated with better physical health, greater resilience, and higher life satisfaction.
suggestions: Continue nurturing your optimistic outlook through positive social connections and meaningful activities
Your optimism is a valuable psychological resource; share it with others
Maintain realistic expectations alongside your positive outlook to make well-informed decisions
